questions.q: What is the core concept of social justice?
opts: (A) Fair distribution of resources
(B) Equality before the law only
(C) Elimination of social inequalities
(D) Protection of individual rights
questions.explanation: Social justice emphasizes the elimination of social inequalities, not just equal treatment under the law.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of institutionalized inequality?
opts: (A) Economic inequality due to unequal pay for the same work
(B) A law that prohibits discrimination in employment
(C) Reservation of seats in educational institutions for marginalized groups
(D) A policy that provides equal access to education for all
questions.explanation: Institutionalized inequality refers to policies or laws that perpetuate inequality, such as unequal pay for the same work.

questions.q: What is the main difference between de facto and de jure segregation?
opts: (A) De facto segregation is intentional, while de jure segregation is unintentional
(B) De facto segregation is unintentional, while de jure segregation is intentional
(C) De facto segregation refers to physical separation, while de jure segregation refers to social separation
(D) De facto segregation refers to economic inequality, while de jure segregation refers to social inequality
questions.explanation: De jure segregation is intentional, while de facto segregation is unintentional.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a just society?
opts: (A) Everyone has equal access to resources
(B) Everyone has an equal chance to succeed
(C) Everyone is treated equally under the law
(D) All of the above
questions.topic: Key Features of a Just Society
questions.explanation: A just society is characterized by equal access to resources, equal opportunities, and equal treatment under the law.

questions.q: What is the primary goal of affirmative action policies?
opts: (A) To provide equal access to opportunities for all
(B) To compensate for historical injustices
(C) To reduce economic inequality
(D) To promote social equality
questions.explanation: Affirmative action policies aim to compensate for historical injustices and promote equality of opportunity.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a structural inequality?
opts: (A) Unequal pay for the same work
(B) Limited access to education for marginalized groups
(C) A law that prohibits discrimination in employment
(D) A policy that provides equal access to healthcare for all
questions.explanation: Structural inequality refers to systemic barriers that limit opportunities and perpetuate inequality, such as limited access to education.

questions.q: What is the concept of intersectionality in social justice?
opts: (A) The idea that individuals have multiple identities that intersect to shape their experiences
(B) The idea that individuals have a single identity that defines their experiences
(C) The idea that social inequalities are separate and distinct
(D) The idea that social inequalities are universal and apply to all
questions.explanation: Intersectionality refers to the idea that individuals have multiple identities that intersect to shape their experiences and create unique forms of oppression.

questions.q: Which of the following is a form of resistance to social injustice?
opts: (A) Silence and complacency
(B) Non-violent protest and activism
(C) Violent protest and revolution
(D) Conformity to societal norms
questions.explanation: Non-violent protest and activism are forms of resistance to social injustice, as they aim to challenge and change unjust systems and policies.

questions.q: What is the concept of social capital in social justice?
opts: (A) The resources and networks that individuals and communities possess
(B) The lack of resources and networks that individuals and communities possess
(C) The social norms and values that govern behavior
(D) The economic inequality between individuals and groups
questions.explanation: Social capital refers to the resources and networks that individuals and communities possess, which can be used to promote social justice and equality.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a just economy?
opts: (A) Everyone has equal access to resources
(B) Everyone has an equal chance to succeed
(C) Everyone is treated equally under the law
(D) All of the above
questions.topic: Key Features of a Just Economy
questions.explanation: A just economy is characterized by equal access to resources, equal opportunities, and equal treatment under the law.

questions.q: What is the primary goal of human rights?
opts: (A) To promote social justice and equality
(B) To protect individual rights and freedoms
(C) To promote economic development
(D) To reduce poverty and inequality
questions.explanation: Human rights aim to protect individual rights and freedoms, which are essential for promoting social justice and equality.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a human rights violation?
opts: (A) Torture and ill-treatment
(B) Unfair trial and lack of due process
(C) Freedom of speech and expression
(D) Right to education and healthcare
questions.explanation: Torture and ill-treatment are examples of human rights violations, as they involve the deliberate infliction of pain and suffering on individuals.

questions.q: What is the concept of distributive justice in social justice?
opts: (A) The fair distribution of resources and benefits
(B) The unequal distribution of resources and benefits
(C) The absence of social inequality
(D) The presence of social inequality
questions.explanation: Distributive justice refers to the fair distribution of resources and benefits, which is essential for promoting social justice and equality.

questions.q: Which of the following is a form of solidarity in social justice?
opts: (A) Supporting marginalized groups through activism and advocacy
(B) Opposing marginalized groups through activism and advocacy
(C) Ignoring marginalized groups and their struggles
(D) Condemning marginalized groups for their actions
questions.explanation: Solidarity involves supporting marginalized groups through activism and advocacy, which helps to promote social justice and equality.

questions.q: What is the concept of social justice pedagogy in education?
opts: (A) The use of education to promote social justice and equality
(B) The use of education to maintain social inequality
(C) The absence of social justice in education
(D) The presence of social justice in education
questions.explanation: Social justice pedagogy involves using education to promote social justice and equality, which is essential for creating a more just and equitable society.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a just global order?
opts: (A) Everyone has equal access to resources and opportunities
(B) Everyone has an equal chance to succeed
(C) Everyone is treated equally under the law
(D) All of the above
questions.topic: Key Features of a Just Global Order
questions.explanation: A just global order is characterized by equal access to resources and opportunities, equal chances to succeed, and equal treatment under the law.

questions.q: What is the primary goal of sustainable development in social justice?
opts: (A) To promote economic growth and development
(B) To protect the environment and promote social justice
(C) To reduce poverty and inequality
(D) To promote human rights and dignity
questions.explanation: Sustainable development aims to protect the environment and promote social justice, which is essential for creating a more equitable and sustainable world.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a sustainable development goal?
opts: (A) To eradicate poverty and hunger
(B) To promote economic growth and development
(C) To protect the environment and promote social justice
(D) To promote human rights and dignity
questions.explanation: Eradicating poverty and hunger is an example of a sustainable development goal, as it aims to promote human well-being and dignity.

questions.q: What is the concept of human security in social justice?
opts: (A) The protection of human rights and dignity
(B) The protection of national security and interests
(C) The absence of human security
(D) The presence of human security
questions.explanation: Human security involves the protection of human rights and dignity, which is essential for promoting social justice and equality.

questions.q: Which of the following is a form of human security?
opts: (A) Food security and nutrition
(B) Economic security and stability
(C) Environmental security and sustainability
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Human security encompasses food security and nutrition, economic security and stability, and environmental security and sustainability, among other forms.
